channel.title: yourfdc.au News — Family Day Care Australia
channel.description: Australia's dedicated Family Day Care news hub.

item.title: Investigation reported after child leaves NSW centre in Cameron Park unsupervised
item.description: An investigation is reportedly underway after a child was able to leave Busy Bees Cameron Park unsupervised, renewing sector focus on supervision systems, risk management and regulatory compliance across early childhood education and care (ECEC) services.
item.content:encoded: An investigation is reportedly underway after a child was able to leave Busy Bees Cameron Park unsupervised, renewing sector focus on supervision systems, risk management and regulatory compliance across early childhood education and care (ECEC) services.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: TeamKids and Dairy Farmers Partner to Reduce Screen Time and Boost Children’s Activity
item.description: The Australian Government has released a dedicated support page for G8 Education Limited workers and their families, following the provider’s decision to suspend a number of early childhood education and care (ECEC) services across Australia.
item.content:encoded: The Australian Government has released a dedicated support page for G8 Education Limited workers and their families, following the provider’s decision to suspend a number of early childhood education and care (ECEC) services across Australia.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: South Australia to establish new integrated hubs for children most in need
item.description: South Australia will open three new integrated child and family hubs, giving families easier access to early education, health and wellbeing services in one location.
item.content:encoded: South Australia will open three new integrated child and family hubs, giving families easier access to early education, health and wellbeing services in one location.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Global summit highlights the economic stakes of early childhood education
item.description: A national summit in the United States has placed early childhood education at the centre of economic debate, reinforcing the argument that investment in the early years is not only a social policy priority, but a driver of workforce participation, productivity and long-term economic stability.
item.content:encoded: A national summit in the United States has placed early childhood education at the centre of economic debate, reinforcing the argument that investment in the early years is not only a social policy priority, but a driver of workforce participation, productivity and long-term economic stability.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Northern Territory Education and Care Awards open for 2026
item.description: The Northern Territory Education and Care Awards have opened for 2026, recognising educators, leaders, services and programs making a positive difference for children and families across the Territory.
item.content:encoded: The Northern Territory Education and Care Awards have opened for 2026, recognising educators, leaders, services and programs making a positive difference for children and families across the Territory.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: What the new Australian Tertiary Education Commission means for the ECEC workforce - The Sector
item.description: What the new Australian Tertiary Education Commission means for the ECEC workforce - The Sector
item.content:encoded: What the new Australian Tertiary Education Commission means for the ECEC workforce - The Sector
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Victoria sets new benchmark with major pay rise for teachers and early childhood educators
item.description: Victoria is set to become one of the highest-paying jurisdictions in Australia for teachers, principals and education support staff, following a significant pay offer from the Allan Government.
item.content:encoded: Victoria is set to become one of the highest-paying jurisdictions in Australia for teachers, principals and education support staff, following a significant pay offer from the Allan Government.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Federal Government Signals Major Overhaul of Australia’s Early Learning System - businessnews.com.au
item.description: Federal Government Signals Major Overhaul of Australia’s Early Learning System - businessnews.com.au
item.content:encoded: Federal Government Signals Major Overhaul of Australia’s Early Learning System - businessnews.com.au
item.dc:creator: businessnews.com.au

item.title: To improve children’s mental health, start by supporting their parents
item.description: Long-term data shows children are more likely to develop mental health issues when their parents experience financial stress and housing instability.
item.content:encoded: Long-term data shows children are more likely to develop mental health issues when their parents experience financial stress and housing instability.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.content:encoded: The Professional Educator Workshop: setting diploma trainees up for success - The Sector
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Leadership Calls for Real Action on Child Safety
item.description: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander leaders call for real action on child safety; warn NT law changes undermine Child Placement Principle.
item.content:encoded: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander leaders call for real action on child safety; warn NT law changes undermine Child Placement Principle.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: SNAICC Raises Alarm Over Narrow NT Child Protection Inquiry Scope
item.description: SNAICC says narrow scope of NT Child Protection Inquiry fail to address systemic issues needed to keep Aboriginal children and families safe.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC says narrow scope of NT Child Protection Inquiry fail to address systemic issues needed to keep Aboriginal children and families safe.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Is baby talk bad? Why ‘parentese’ actually helps babies learn language
item.description: Exaggerating phrases and talking in a sing-song way can actually help, not make it harder, for young children to master speaking a language.
item.content:encoded: Exaggerating phrases and talking in a sing-song way can actually help, not make it harder, for young children to master speaking a language.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 15 May 2026
item.description: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, the Federal Budget was a missed opportunity for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities. NT Government has introduced legislation that undermine the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Child Placement Principle. And a heartwarming Mother's Day story from a member of SNAICC's Youth Voice advisory group on how love, kinship and culture can help children in out-of-home-care grow up Deadly.
item.content:encoded: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, the Federal Budget was a missed opportunity for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities. NT Government has introduced legislation that undermine the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Child Placement Principle. And a heartwarming Mother's Day story from a member of SNAICC's Youth Voice advisory group on how love, kinship and culture can help children in out-of-home-care grow up Deadly.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Budget Falls Short for Aboriginal Children Despite Community-Led Solutions on the Table
item.description: Budget 2026 missed opportunity for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander children and families with community-led solutions on the table.

item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Sector Peak Urges NT Government to Scrap Legislative Changes and Partner on Child Protection
item.description: SNAICC is urging the NT Government to abandon proposed child protection legislative changes that would weaken the Child Placement Principle.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC is urging the NT Government to abandon proposed child protection legislative changes that would weaken the Child Placement Principle.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 1 May 2026
item.description: This is not the news we wanted to hear. This week has been a tragedy that has shattered the nation and devastated the community of Mparntwe/Alice Springs.
item.content:encoded: This is not the news we wanted to hear. This week has been a tragedy that has shattered the nation and devastated the community of Mparntwe/Alice Springs.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: National Child and Family Investment Strategy sets clear direction, now governments must deliver
item.description: SNAICC has welcomed the release of the National Child and Family Investment Strategy as a landmark framework to shift investment to ACCOs.
